commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `command` cron payload type across protocol validation, cron persistence/normalization, CLI add/edit, gateway execution/delivery, Control UI preservation/display, tests, and docs.

PR surface: Source +885, Tests +571, Docs +62. Total +1518 across 37 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR. Source inspection confirms current main lacks command cron payload support, while the PR adds that new capability.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is required because the PR is draft/protected, adds a security-sensitive scheduled host-command surface, and lacks required real behavior proof.

target.title: [codex] support command cron jobs
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

Scheduled shell-style cron work currently has to go through an isolated agent/model run, which adds setup pressure and makes simple command jobs more expensive than they need to be.

Why does this matter now?

Gateway cron pressure is already visible in production-style scheduled workloads. The right short-term fix is to stagger existing jobs, but OpenClaw also needs a first-class path for jobs that are command execution rather than agent reasoning.

What is the intended outcome?

Command cron jobs can be stored, edited, and executed by the gateway scheduler as command payloads, with stdout/stderr capture, timeout handling, output limits, failure reporting, and `NO_REPLY` suppression.

What is intentionally out of scope?

Command authoring remains CLI-first in this PR. The UI preserves and displays existing command cron jobs, but it does not add a full command-job authoring flow. This PR also does not change deployed gateway configuration or existing cron schedules.

What does success look like?

Existing agent cron jobs keep working, command cron payloads round-trip through schema/store/CLI/UI surfaces, and the gateway cron runner executes command jobs without starting an isolated agent/model run.

What should reviewers focus on?

Cron payload compatibility, command execution boundaries, output suppression semantics, timeout/error behavior, and whether the CLI/API surface is narrow enough for this first step.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No closing issue yet. Candidate canonical tracker is #18160, but this PR leaves it as related until maintainers decide whether CLI/API-first command jobs are enough to close the broader direct-exec cron request.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #18160, #62789, #64598, #69455, #61152, #63107, #82662, #84567, #86893, #89055, #89442.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Owner/maintainer requested in response to production cron pressure and the need to run shell-style scheduled work inside OpenClaw instead of as separate OS cron jobs.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Scheduled command jobs can be stored and executed as command payloads by the gateway cron runner, including stdout/stderr capture, timeout handling, output limits, and `NO_REPLY` suppression.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS; no deployed gateway was modified.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/normalize.test.ts src/cron/command-runner.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/cron-validators.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`; `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt 'Only report P0/P1 correctness, data-loss, security, or production-breakage findings. Omit P2/P3 findings entirely.'`; `pnpm build`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/command-runner.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-edit.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.test.ts src/cron/normalize.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts`; `pnpm format:check src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-edit.test.ts src/cron/command-runner.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Focused Vitest shards passed: unit 16 tests, gateway 23 tests, cron 89 tests, CLI 87 tests, agents 100 tests, UI 40 tests. Additional comparison-pass shards passed: gateway 23 tests, cron 62 tests, CLI 4 tests. `git diff --check` passed. Focused format check passed for the added regression tests. P0/P1-scoped autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings. `pnpm build` completed successfully.
- Observed result after fix: Command cron payloads validate, persist, normalize, execute, report output, suppress silent output, and round-trip through the tested CLI/store/UI surfaces.
- What was not tested: A live deployed gateway cron run was not executed; GitHub CI has not completed on this PR yet.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Local proof covers the code path and focused contract tests, but not a live production gateway schedule firing on `jpclawhq`.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing scheduled shell-style work had no first-class cron command payload path and had to be represented as agent/model work or moved outside OpenClaw.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/normalize.test.ts src/cron/command-runner.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts packages/gateway-protocol/src/cron-validators.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts`

`git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

`AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt 'Only report P0/P1 correctness, data-loss, security, or production-breakage findings. Omit P2/P3 findings entirely.'`

`pnpm build`

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/command-runner.test.ts src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-edit.test.ts src/cron/service/timer.test.ts src/cron/normalize.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts`; `pnpm format:check src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-edit.test.ts src/cron/command-runner.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Protocol cron validators, cron store/normalization, command runner, gateway cron execution, CLI cron add/edit behavior, agent cron tool rejection of command payloads, and UI cron controller preservation/display coverage. Follow-up coverage pins command timeout/no-output/abort failures as cron errors and keeps delivery-only / timeout-only CLI edits from corrupting existing command payloads.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Command cron payloads were not a supported first-class scheduled job type, so the gateway cron runner could not execute shell-style work directly as a cron command payload.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A. Focused regression coverage was added/updated across the touched surfaces.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. CLI/API cron jobs can now include command payloads, and existing command jobs are preserved/displayed by the UI.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. Existing cron records remain compatible; no deployed config migration is required.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This adds a first-class scheduled command execution path inside the gateway cron runner.

What is the highest-risk area?

Command execution semantics and output handling, especially making sure command jobs are explicit command payloads, bounded by timeout/output limits, and do not accidentally become agent-authored jobs.

How is that risk mitigated?

The implementation keeps command authoring CLI/API scoped, rejects command payloads from the agent cron tool, runs through focused command-runner coverage, caps output, records stderr/stdout, reports failures, and preserves `NO_REPLY` suppression behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and GitHub CI. The PR is currently draft while review/CI state is checked.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on CI and maintainer judgment on whether local proof is sufficient before marking ready. A live deployed gateway cron run has not been performed.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

P0/P1-scoped autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings. ClawSweeper's autocomment timed out before completing a code review, so there are no bot code findings from that comment to address yet.
